items.title: VI Live: toeleven naar middag vol Europese lotingen
items.summary: Vrijdagmiddag wordt er geloot voor de achtste finales in de Champions League, de Europa League en de Conference League. In dat laatste toernooi verdwijnt AZ als enige Nederlandse club in de koker. De Alkmaarders treffen AEK Athene of Sparta Praag in de achtste finale. Via dit liveblog blijf je de hele middag op de hoogte van al het lotinggeweld.

items.title: Ontslagpremies over 100 miljoen bij United na hoog bedrag voor Amorim
items.summary: Manchester United moet Rúben Amorim ruim achttien miljoen euro meegeven na zijn ontslag . Dat is het bedrag waarmee het doorlopende contract van de Portugees en zijn staf is afgekocht. Daarmee staat de teller op ruim honderd miljoen euro aan ontslagpremies van trainers sinds het Alex Ferguson-tijdperk.

items.title: 'Clausule bemoeilijkt Juve-transfer Osimhen, Antonio wordt ploeggenoot El Ghazi'
items.summary: We zitten in een periode tussen twee transferwindows, maar de stroom aan transfernieuws houdt nooit op. Traditiegetrouw volgt Voetbal International de buitenlandse markt op de voet. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: Frenkie de Jong geblesseerd: 'Schouten ideale vervanger'
items.summary: In de nieuwe aflevering van de VI ZSM bespreken Sander Janssen en Sjoerd Keizer de blessure van Frenkie de Jong en wie zijn positie in moet vullen om tot het ideale middenveld te komen zonder De Jong. Ook de rechtsbackpositie komt uitgebreid aan bod.

items.title: Wanneer is loting achtste finale Champions League en waar wordt die uitgezonden?
items.summary: De tussenronde van de Champions League zit erop en dus kunnen we vooruitkijken richting de loting voor de achtste finale. Zestien ploegen doen daaraan mee. In dit artikel alles over de Champions League-loting.

items.title: What could happen in Champions League round-of-16 draw?
items.summary: The draw for the Champions League round of 16 is set for Friday at 6 a.m. ET (11 a.m. GMT) but how does it work and which teams are likely to face each other?

items.title: Forest dodge fireworks before advancing in UEL
items.summary: Nottingham Forest players were forced to dodge fireworks thrown on to the pitch by Fenerbahce fans at the start of their Europa League playoff second leg.

items.title: Messi scores as Miami wins rescheduled friendly
items.summary: Inter Miami CF beat Independiente del Valle 2-1 on Thursday night in Puerto Rico with goals from Santiago Morales and Lionel Messi to conclude the team's preseason friendly tour.

items.title: Klopp's job at risk? 'Nonsense,' says Red Bull
items.summary: Red Bull backed former Liverpool manager Jürgen Klopp to remain the drinks giant's head of global soccer and dismissed a media report that his job could be under threat after barely a year.

items.title: Neymar shushes critics after first goals in return
items.summary: Neymar broke his 2026 goal drought in spectacular fashion Thursday, leading Santos to a 2-1 victory over Vasco da Gama in the fourth round of the Brasileirão.

items.title: Will Aston Villa hold on to their place in the Champions League?
items.summary: Villa’s run of three wins in nine league games has opened the door to Manchester United, Chelsea and Liverpool By WhoScored Two months ago, Aston Villa were just three points off top spot in the Premier League. They were on a run of 12 wins in 14 games that included victories against Manchester City and Arsenal. Their run of eight consecutive wins in the league was their best since they won 10 in a row in 1910. You wouldn’t have blamed Villa fans for daring to dream about lifting their first league title since 1981. But success breeds expectation, and expectation carries its own cruel weight. Arsenal tore Villa apart in a 4-1 win in late December and victories have been hard to come by in the new year – they have only won three of their last nine league games. Liverpool, Manchester United and Chelsea are still below them in the table but the gap is narrowing. With games against United and Chelsea coming in March, the top-three spot Villa have occupied since they beat Wolves in late November suddenly looks in jeopardy. So does their place in the Champions League next season. Continue reading...

items.title: From Hearts failure to Estoril excitement - the 'most Portuguese Scot there is'
items.summary: After a forgettable seven months in charge at Hearts in 2017, Scottish manager Ian Cathro is rebuilding his reputation at Estoril.

items.title: Today on Sky Sports Racing: Ferrous and El Bodon clash in Lingfield Sprint Trial
items.summary: We have a busy day's racing on Friday with action from Doncaster, Lingfield, Newbury and Wolverhampton, live on Sky Sports Racing...

items.title: Brundle: Russell 'ready' for first F1 title challenge
items.summary: George Russell is "ready" to fight for a first F1 world championship in the 2026 season, says Sky Sports F1's Martin Brundle.

items.title: Warren: Dubois' pre-fight party 'can't happen again'
items.summary: The pre-fight ‘party’ that took place ahead of Daniel Dubois’ defeat to Oleksandr Usyk “can’t happen again”, says promoter Frank Warren.

items.title: Premier League: 10 things to look out for this weekend
items.summary: Chelsea must keep their heads at Arsenal, Anthony Gordon faces his old club and a key return for Sunderland Unai Emery has seen most things in this game but he has never won at Wolves. In three and a half years at Villa, he has lost two and drawn one of his three away games at Molineux. Twelve months ago they lost this fixture 2-0 and this week Emery shared his poor record to stress the difficulty of the challenge facing his side, particularly given they have won just one of their past five matches in all competitions. Emery even mentioned his visit to Wolverhampton with Arsenal in 2019, when his team trailed 3-0 at half-time and lost 3-1. For Emery, there is no better time to break his duck, with the schedule dictating that Villa could move nine points clear of fifth-placed Chelsea, who visit Villa on Wednesday, before Liam Rosenior’s side travel to Arsenal on Sunday. Victory would enhance Villa’s chances of returning to the Champions League but also pile pressure on a direct rival. Ben Fisher Wolves v Aston Villa, Friday 8pm (all kick-offs GMT) Bournemouth v Sunderland, Saturday 12.30pm Burnley v Brentford, Saturday 3pm Liverpool v West Ham, Saturday 3pm Continue reading...

items.title: 'Don't know what fuss was about' - chaotic Palace season could end in glory
items.summary: Key players have gone, the manager is leaving and has criticised the club and upset fans - yet Crystal Palace's chaotic campaign could still end in European glory.

items.title: Glasner admits sparking media storm after Crystal Palace ease past Zrinjski
items.summary: It is a peculiarity particular to Crystal Palace that a season of such upheaval and unrest could still end up with Oliver Glasner’s side winning another trophy. Having left the pitch after last week’s first leg with supporters calling for the Austrian manager to be sacked in the morning, Maxence Lacroix and Evann Guessand made it a much more harmonious evening for Glasner and his side. After being fortunate to escape from last week’s trip to Bosnia and Herzegovina with a 1-1 draw, it could have been very different if Zrinjski Mostar had equalised just before Guessand settled the tie late on. But having been demoted from the Europa League to the Conference League after winning the FA Cup last season, Palace’s first European campaign will continue against either the Cypriot side Larnaca – who they lost to during the group stages – or Mainz from Germany in the last 16. Continue reading...

items.title: Sacking Amorim could cost Man Utd nearly £16m
items.summary: Ruben Amorim's 14 months in charge of Manchester United have come at a price, with the club now paying up to £15.9m to sack him, in addition to £11m to hire the Portuguese.

items.title: Hudson-Odoi sends Nottingham Forest into last 16 despite fright by Fenerbahce
items.summary: For 67 minutes, Nottingham Forest were at risk of matching an unwanted record. At that point Fenerbahce were 2-0 up and full of belief that they could achieve the miracle their head coach, Domenico Tedesco, had been manifesting since their 3-0 first-leg defeat in Istanbul. Only one team in the history of European club competitions have lost the first leg of a tie by a three-goal margin at home and advanced in the second. Up in the directors’ box, Evangelos Marinakis, more than an interested party on that occasion, presumably had flashbacks of Olympiakos suffering a 4-1 home defeat by Maccabi Tel Aviv in the Conference League two years ago, only to triumph 6-1 in Serbia in the second leg. Continue reading...
